title: When you stop eating may matter more than what you eat for better sleep
author: Ellsworth Toohey
summary: A three-hour buffer between your last meal and bedtime improved circadian rhythms, blood pressure, heart rate, and blood sugar, according to neurologist Phyllis Zee. She ran a clinical trial at Northwestern showing that "Perhaps more than what you eat, when you eat relative to sleep may be as important," Zee said, according to the Washington Post. —
